items.title: 'Everything had been looted': Myanmar's civil war takes heavy toll
Trump says he spoke with Putin about a possible ceasefire in Ukraine
items.description: President Trump said he talked to Russian President Putin Wednesday about the wars in Ukraine and Iran.

items.title: Lebanon’s ex-president Amin Gemayel says time is right for direct talks with Israel
items.description: 'We have to see how far we can go' in negotiations, says man who led Lebanon during its civil war, adding that his people 'yearn for the bare minimum of calm, peace, stability' The post Lebanon’s ex-president Amin Gemayel says time is right for direct talks with Israel appeared first on The Times of Israel.

items.title: Oil price tops $120 a barrel after Trump warns Iran blockade could last ‘months’
items.description: Oil markets spooked as US president appears willing to maintain US Navy blockade and Iran keeps strait of Hormuz all but shut The price of Brent oil soared above $126 a barrel on Wednesday, its highest level since 2022, after Donald Trump warned the US blockade of Iranian ports could last months and peace talks remaine…

items.title: Putin chiama Trump: 'Dare una chance ai negoziati con l'Iran'
items.description: Colloquio di un'ora e mezza tra i due presidenti. Lo zar apre a una tregua con Kiev nel Giorno della Vittoria

items.title: Video emerges of heavy firefight in Mali
items.description: Video has been published by Russian mercenaries showing a heavy gun battle between Mali’s military and rebels.

items.title: Assad regime trials are 'integral for Syria to move on' from civil war, atrocities
items.description: A Syrian court conducted the first hearing Sunday in the trial of ousted ruler Bashar al-Assad and senior figures from his government, one of whom appeared in person. Speaking with FRANCE 24's Sharon Gaffney, Nanar Hawach, Senior Analyst for Syria at the International Crisis Group, says that despite most perpetrators b…

items.title: Kremlin: Putin Wants Victory Day Truce
items.description: Vladimir Putin discussed a possible Victory Day truce with Donald Trump, despite past Russian ceasefire pledges that Ukraine says were repeatedly violated.

items.title: What are the sticking points for the EU's new budget?
items.description: The battle over the next long-term EU budget has begun. The EU's institutions have staked out their positions and are seeking significantly more funding, but it is the 27 member states that will ultimately decide.

items.title: Putin, Trump Discuss Iran, Ukraine in Phone Call, Kremlin Aide Says
items.description: Putin offered a ceasefire in the fighting in Ukraine on May 9, when Russia celebrates the Soviet Union's World War II victory over Nazi Germany.

items.title: Malian jihadists and rebels ramp up pressure on junta: 'We are blocking Bamako'
items.description: Jihadists from the Al Qaeda-affiliated group JNIM announced a total siege of the Malian capital on Tuesday. At least five towns in the country's north have fallen to the attackers. 'The situation is under control,' President Assimi Goita said.

items.title: Kim Jong Un praises troops who 'self-blasted' to avoid capture by Ukraine
items.description: It confirms suspicions that North Korean soldiers are being told to detonate their grenades to kill themselves.

items.title: Their nation at war, Israelis still demand to know: How could Oct. 7 happen?
items.description: Amid wars with Iran and Hezbollah, Israelis still very much want an independent state commission of inquiry to learn the truth about the failure to prevent the Hamas attack that sparked the Gaza war. Israel’s top court has given the Netanyahu government more time.

items.title: North Korea Is Honoring Some Of Its Soldiers Killed In The Ukraine War. But What About The Rest?
items.description: Why a sprawling war memorial that was opened on April 26 commemorates only a fraction of the estimated thousands of troops Pyongyang lost in Russia.

items.title: SECURITY COUNCIL LIVE: Gaza ceasefire is ‘increasingly fragile’, UN warns
items.description: The Security Council turns again to the Middle East crisis in New York on Tuesday, with debate focusing on the fragile Gaza ceasefire, a worsening humanitarian crisis there and efforts to advance a US-backed peace plan.UN senior political affairs official Khaled Khiari warned of increasingly dire conditions in Gaza and…
